title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Redirects, Parser Limits, and Output Injection · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs ssrf, dns rebinding, redirects, parser limits, and output injection from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for untrusted-input security boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Allowlist schemes and destinations, reject credentials and dangerous address classes, constrain ports, and separate user input from fetch authority.
Resolve and classify every connection destination at the correct time, bind policy to the connected peer where possible, and recheck changes.
Apply full destination, credential, DNS, port, method, and budget policy on every redirect and browser subrequest.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

lines.label: assumption

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Encode HTML reports, neutralize spreadsheet formulas, validate URLs, escape logs, and keep untrusted content out of commands and templates. Artifact under review: untrusted-input security boundary.

options.text: The Output Injection Defense record says untrusted-input security boundary looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Output Injection Defense.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Output Injection Defense for untrusted-input security boundary.
The record names the assumption, applies Output Injection Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
